title: elaeocarpus subcapitatus
intro: Elaeocarpus subcapitatus, also known as the mountain blueberry or blue olive tree, is a small tree native to Australia. Its leaves, bark, and fruit have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus subcapitatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and applications.
